{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What level of intervention do you want? Choose one: (A) Light copyedit only (grammar, wording, minor clarity; no reordering or reframing of claims), (B) Substantive rewrite (allow paragraph-/section-level reorganization and reframing/tempering of contribution and novelty language while preserving technical content), or (C) Deep revision (also verify and recalibrate novelty/“first” claims against specific prior work and add/adjust citations).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Clarifying the high-level thesis and contribution claims will likely be needed to make the draft’s core message crisp beyond sentence-level tweaks.",
    "R1.2": "The argument that the combined strategy is first, commitment-preserving, and LP-feasible requires checking internal logical coherence across premises and conclusions.",
    "R1.5": "Reordering and tightening the section-level flow (baseline → combined method → pipeline and evaluation context) can materially improve the narrative arc.",
    "R1.6": "Polishing sentence- and paragraph-level wording for clarity, concision, and register is the primary expectation for ‘improve this draft.’",
    "RX.1": "Strong assertions (e.g., “first,” “deployment-ready”) should be qualified to disclose uncertainty where verification is incomplete.",
    "RX.2": "Novelty and performance claims must be carefully tempered to avoid overclaiming beyond what is supported in the provided text.",
    "RX.3": "Edits should remain within the scope of improving this excerpt without introducing new experimental results or external content.",
    "RX.4": "Language should avoid encouraging unsafe or overconfident operational use in real deployments without proper validation.",
    "RX.5": "Revisions should keep claims traceable to the source text and clearly indicate where additional evidence or citations would be required."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What level of intervention do you want? Choose one: (A) Light copyedit only (grammar, wording, minor clarity; no reordering or reframing of claims), (B) Substantive rewrite (allow paragraph-/section-level reorganization and reframing/tempering of contribution and novelty language while preserving technical content), or (C) Deep revision (also verify and recalibrate novelty/“first” claims against specific prior work and add/adjust citations).

rationale.R1.1: Clarifying the high-level thesis and contribution claims will likely be needed to make the draft’s core message crisp beyond sentence-level tweaks.
rationale.R1.2: The argument that the combined strategy is first, commitment-preserving, and LP-feasible requires checking internal logical coherence across premises and conclusions.
rationale.R1.5: Reordering and tightening the section-level flow (baseline → combined method → pipeline and evaluation context) can materially improve the narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing sentence- and paragraph-level wording for clarity, concision, and register is the primary expectation for ‘improve this draft.’
rationale.RX.1: Strong assertions (e.g., “first,” “deployment-ready”) should be qualified to disclose uncertainty where verification is incomplete.
rationale.RX.2: Novelty and performance claims must be carefully tempered to avoid overclaiming beyond what is supported in the provided text.
rationale.RX.3: Edits should remain within the scope of improving this excerpt without introducing new experimental results or external content.
rationale.RX.4: Language should avoid encouraging unsafe or overconfident operational use in real deployments without proper validation.
rationale.RX.5: Revisions should keep claims traceable to the source text and clearly indicate where additional evidence or citations would be required.
